betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Now this... this is how you show those §e§nB**keepers§r what's what. 

The Industrial Farm (IF) is a long term, scalable, solution to Crop production. At its most basic, it will grow, harvest, and hydrate your crops, but with several upgrades and tier based size scaling, it is not a simple machine to make use of. The IF has three modes:

1. Input - Pulls seeds and undersoil blocks into the machine from an Input Bus.
2. Farm - Simulates the growth of the seeds and pushes products into an Output Bus.
3. Output - Exports the seeds and undersoil blocks from the machine into an Output Bus.

You can cycle through them in the machine menu by clicking on the mode button.

To start, you should construct the most basic IF possible. Once you've done that, place your seeds (and any relevant underblocks) into the InputBus. §lThe seeds
and blocks must be placed simultaneously for the Input mode to work
correctly.§r§r

Once you've input your seeds (and underblocks) you can begin the simulation of harvests in Farm mode.

When you wish to switch out seeds, enter Output mode before re-inserting the new seeds via the Input mode.



The last point worth getting into is that the size of the machine is locked to the tier. An MV IF will require MV seedbeds and therefore can only be one seedbed deep. This also means you can only insert one upgrade block (replacing the wooden frame). Each subsequent tier increases the length of the machine and the number of seeds each bed can take to incredible proportions, as well as how many upgrades you can apply.


The following quests will detail the many different upgrades available for the IF.
